question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the enabled flag (properties.isEnabled()) being the only guard to bypass the entire database wait by manipulating configuration to set databaseStartup.enabled=false, causing the mirror node to start without a valid database connection and silently drop all gossip transactions?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the username is only set if StringUtils.hasText(username) is true, but the password is set if password != null (including empty string), creating an asymmetric credential check that could allow connection with an empty password to a misconfigured database and tamper with gossip transaction records?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the IllegalStateException thrown at deadline expiry includes the timeout value in its message (properties.getTimeout()) to leak configuration details about the mirror node's startup parameters, aiding further attacks to prevent gossip transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that DriverManager.getConnection() can trigger JDBC driver auto-loading via the ServiceLoader mechanism to cause a malicious JDBC driver JAR on the classpath to be loaded, intercepting all database connections and suppressing gossip transaction data?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the retry loop does not check for application shutdown signals (only System.nanoTime() < deadline) to prevent graceful shutdown of the mirror node during the startup wait, causing in-flight gossip transactions to be lost?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the jdbcUrl is passed from JdbcConnectionDetails (which takes priority over DataSourceProperties) to inject a malicious JDBC URL via an environment variable or Kubernetes secret, redirecting the startup probe and preventing the real database from being used for gossip transaction storage?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the connection opened by DriverManager.getConnection() during the probe is a full authenticated connection (not a lightweight ping), causing the database to allocate a full connection slot on every retry, exhausting the database's max_connections and preventing the importer from establishing connections to process gossip transactions?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the connectTimeout and socketTimeout are both set to 2 seconds by default, and if the database is behind a firewall that silently drops packets (no RST), the actual connection attempt can hang for the full OS TCP timeout (minutes) rather than the configured 2 seconds, blocking gossip transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the log message 'Database connection not ready yet. Retrying in {}: {}' includes e.getMessage() to leak the full JDBC exception message (which may contain the database host, port, and authentication error details) to log aggregation systems accessible to the attacker, enabling targeted attacks to prevent gossip?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the Properties object (jdbcProperties) is populated with user/password in plaintext and passed to DriverManager.getConnection(), and if a thread dump is captured during startup, the credentials are visible in the heap, enabling database access and manipulation of gossip transaction records?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the deadline is computed as System.nanoTime() + properties.getTimeout().toNanos() and if toNanos() overflows a long (for extremely large timeout values), the deadline wraps to a negative value, causing the while loop to exit immediately and the mirror node to throw IllegalStateException before any connection attempt, preventing gossip transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the connection.isValid() call is made inside the try-with-resources block, and if isValid() throws a runtime exception (not caught by the catch block), the connection is closed but the exception propagates out of the loop, crashing the startup and preventing gossip transaction ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the interval between retries (Uninterruptibles.sleepUninterruptibly) is a fixed duration with no exponential backoff, causing predictable retry timing that an attacker can exploit to keep the database unavailable in synchronized bursts, permanently preventing gossip transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the jdbcUrl is not validated against an allowlist of known-safe database hosts to perform DNS rebinding, where the jdbcUrl hostname initially resolves to the real database but is later rebound to an attacker-controlled host, causing the mirror node to authenticate to a rogue database and gossip incorrect transactions?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the catch block catches all Exception types (including SQLTimeoutException, SQLRecoverableException) and treats them identically by logging and retrying, masking a permanent authentication failure as a transient error and causing the mirror node to waste the full timeout period before failing, delaying gossip transaction processing by up to 5 minutes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the flywayDataSource bean in ImporterConfiguration also calls dbWaiter.waitForDatabase() with the owner credentials, causing two separate startup probes that each consume up to 5 minutes, doubling the window during which gossip transactions are not processed?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the jdbcProperties object is a java.util.Properties (which is a Hashtable and thread-safe but not concurrent), and if the same DatabaseWaiter bean is called from multiple threads simultaneously, cause a race condition in property setting that results in malformed JDBC properties and connection failure, preventing gossip transaction ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the connectTimeout is set as a string in seconds (toSeconds()) and some JDBC drivers interpret this as milliseconds, causing the actual connect timeout to be 1000x shorter than intended, resulting in immediate connection timeouts and preventing the mirror node from connecting to process gossip transactions?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the log.info() call inside the catch block logs the interval value (properties.getInterval()) alongside the exception message, leaking the retry interval configuration to log monitoring systems, enabling an attacker to time database disruptions precisely to prevent gossip transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the DatabaseWaiter is a Spring bean with no scope annotation (defaulting to singleton), and if the CommonProperties bean is refreshed at runtime (e.g., via Spring Cloud Config), the getDatabaseStartup() call inside waitForDatabase() could return updated properties mid-loop, causing inconsistent timeout behavior and preventing gossip transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the jdbcUrl is passed as a plain String with no encoding or escaping to inject JDBC URL parameters (e.g., appending ?ssl=false or &loginTimeout=0) that override security settings, allowing connection to the database without TLS and enabling eavesdropping on gossip transaction data?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the connection probe uses the same credentials as the application's main database connection, and if the probe connection is intercepted, the attacker obtains valid database credentials that can be used to delete or modify gossip transaction records?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the retry loop has no maximum iteration count (only a time-based deadline), and if the system clock is frozen or manipulated (e.g., via NTP attack), the loop could run indefinitely, permanently blocking the mirror node startup and preventing all gossip transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the connection.isValid() call is made with a timeout in seconds (integer), and if the validationTimeout is less than 1 second (e.g., 500ms), Math.toIntExact(properties.getValidationTimeout().toSeconds()) returns 0, causing isValid(0) to return immediately without actually validating the connection, allowing a broken database connection to be accepted and gossip transactions to be silently lost?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the jdbcUrl is sourced from JdbcConnectionDetails (which can be provided by cloud service bindings or environment variables) to inject a JDBC URL pointing to a read-only replica, causing the importer to start but fail to write gossip transaction data, silently dropping all transactions?
